SEARCH
Package com.dalegames.highlowjack.model

Class Game

java.lang.Object
com.dalegames.highlowjack.model.Game
public class Game extends Object
Represents the complete state of a High Low Jack game.

Manages the game flow including dealing cards, playing tricks, tracking scores, and determining the winner. Supports exactly 4 players.

Game flow:

  1. Create game with 4 player names
  2. Deal 7 cards to each player
  3. First card played determines trump suit
  4. Play 7 tricks
  5. Score High, Low, Jack, Game points
  6. First to 7 total points wins
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dale & Primus

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game(List<String> playerNames)
      Constructs a new game with the specified player names.
      Parameters:
      playerNames - exactly 4 player names
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if not exactly 4 players or any name is null/empty

    • Game

      public Game(String player1, String player2, String player3, String player4)
      Convenience constructor for 4 individual player names.
      Parameters:
      player1 - first player's name
      player2 - second player's name
      player3 - third player's name
      player4 - fourth player's name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any name is null/empty

  • Method Details

    • dealCards

      public void dealCards()
      Deals cards to all players and starts the game. Creates a new shuffled deck and deals 7 cards to each player.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if game is already in progress

    • playCard

      public Trick playCard(Card card)
      Plays a card for the current player.

      The first card played in the first trick determines the trump suit. Players must follow suit if possible. When a trick is complete (4 cards), the winner is determined and becomes the next player to lead.

      Parameters:
      card - the card to play
      Returns:
      the current trick after playing the card
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if game is not in progress or no cards dealt
      IllegalArgumentException - if card is not in player's hand or play is invalid

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public String getCurrentPlayer()
      Returns the name of the current player whose turn it is.
      Returns:
      the current player's name

    • getHand

      public Hand getHand(String playerName)
      Returns the hand for the specified player.
      Parameters:
      playerName - the player's name
      Returns:
      the player's hand
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if player name is not in game

    • getPlayerNames

      public List<String> getPlayerNames()
      Returns all player names in turn order.
      Returns:
      a copy of the player names list

    • getTrumpSuit

      public Card.Suit getTrumpSuit()
      Returns the current trump suit, or null if not yet determined. Trump is set by the first card played in the game.
      Returns:
      the trump suit, or null if game hasn't started

    • getTricks

      public List<Trick> getTricks()
      Returns all completed tricks in the order they were played.
      Returns:
      a copy of the tricks list

    • getCurrentTrick

      public Trick getCurrentTrick()
      Returns the current trick being played, or null if between tricks.
      Returns:
      the current trick, or null

    • getState

      public Game.GameState getState()
      Returns the current game state.
      Returns:
      the current state

    • getScores

      public Map<String,Integer> getScores()
      Returns a copy of the current scores map.
      Returns:
      map of player names to their scores

    • getScore

      public int getScore(String playerName)
      Returns the score for a specific player.
      Parameters:
      playerName - the player's name
      Returns:
      the player's current score
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if player name is not in game

    • addScore

      public void addScore(String playerName, int points)
      Adds points to a player's score.
      Parameters:
      playerName - the player's name
      points - the points to add
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if player name is not in game or points is negative

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns whether the game is over (a player has reached winning score).
      Returns:
      true if game is over

    • getWinner

      public String getWinner()
      Returns the winner of the game, or null if game is not over.
      Returns:
      the winning player's name, or null

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the current game state.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      formatted game state including players, scores, and current status
